description: Compact domain bootstrap terms for AI assistants used with GENtle.

terms.definition: The HGNC is the authority for approved human gene nomenclature. It assigns approved human gene symbols, approved gene names, and stable HGNC IDs.
terms.prompt_hint: Treat HGNC symbols such as TP53 or FUS as approved human gene symbols, but treat HGNC IDs such as HGNC:11998 as nomenclature-record identifiers that may need resolution before using Ensembl, GenBank, or UniProt fetch commands.

terms.definition: Stable identifier assigned by the HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee to one approved human gene-symbol record. The format is HGNC:n, for example HGNC:11998.
terms.prompt_hint: Do not pass an HGNC ID directly to GenBank or UniProt fetch routes. If a user provides an HGNC ID, first resolve it to an approved symbol or linked external accession before suggesting a database-specific GENtle fetch command.

terms.definition: NCBI nucleotide sequence archive and flat-file record format used for accessions such as NM_, NC_, NG_, or other GenBank nucleotide records.
terms.prompt_hint: For GENtle `genbank fetch ACCESSION`, ask for an exact NCBI nucleotide accession; `--as-id` or slash-alias `--id` names the local GENtle sequence created from that record.

terms.definition: Genome annotation resource with species-specific REST endpoints and stable identifiers for genes, transcripts, proteins/translations, and genomic regions.
terms.prompt_hint: Use species names such as homo_sapiens. For `ensembl-gene fetch`, QUERY can be a gene symbol or stable Ensembl gene id; `--entry-id` names the local GENtle metadata entry.

terms.definition: Protein knowledgebase; GENtle fetches Swiss-Prot text entries for protein evidence and projection workflows.
terms.prompt_hint: For GENtle `uniprot fetch QUERY`, use a UniProt accession or entry name such as P04637 or P53_HUMAN; `--entry-id` names the local GENtle metadata entry.

terms.definition: Carrier DNA backbone used to host an insert.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for vector name, host context, and required cloning constraints.

terms.definition: Sequence fragment intended to be introduced into a vector.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for exact sequence ID or coordinate range.

terms.definition: Coding sequence expected to preserve reading frame.
terms.prompt_hint: Confirm strand/orientation before frame-sensitive suggestions.

terms.definition: Annotated genomic unit that may include multiple transcripts.
terms.prompt_hint: If transcript ambiguity exists, ask user to disambiguate.

terms.definition: RNA gene or transcript whose functional product is RNA rather than a translated protein.
terms.prompt_hint: Do not assume a CDS or protein product. For cloning or expression planning, ask whether the user wants the RNA locus, mature RNA, precursor RNA, promoter, or assay target.

terms.definition: Short regulatory non-coding RNA, commonly represented as a precursor hairpin and one or more mature miRNA products.
terms.prompt_hint: Clarify whether the user means the MIR gene locus, precursor hairpin, or mature miRNA sequence before suggesting extraction, cloning, or target-site commands.

terms.definition: Long non-coding RNA transcript, often at least 200 nucleotides, with no intended protein-coding product.
terms.prompt_hint: For lncRNA/lincRNA requests, avoid protein-gel or CDS assumptions unless explicit coding evidence is provided; ask for transcript/locus/promoter intent.

terms.definition: Regulatory region influencing transcription initiation.
terms.prompt_hint: Confirm desired upstream/downstream orientation and strand context.

terms.definition: Recognition sequence for a restriction endonuclease.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for required and forbidden enzyme sites.

terms.definition: Single-stranded end produced or designed for assembly.
terms.prompt_hint: Confirm compatible overhang directionality before ligation suggestions.

terms.definition: Joining DNA ends into one continuous molecule.
terms.prompt_hint: Use as conceptual step; keep recommendations in-silico unless requested.

terms.definition: Overlap-based DNA assembly method.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for overlap length and fragment order if assembly planning is needed.

terms.definition: Type IIS restriction-based modular assembly method.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for enzyme choice and forbidden internal sites.

terms.definition: In-sequence positional reference used to define local intervals.
terms.prompt_hint: Use for between-anchor extraction and candidate generation on one sequence.

terms.definition: Provenance mapping of a sequence to assembly coordinates.
terms.prompt_hint: Required for genome track import and genome extension workflows.

terms.definition: Explicit set of windows/subsequences with per-candidate metrics.
terms.prompt_hint: Prefer set algebra and explicit filters instead of ad-hoc ranking prose.

terms.definition: Composite score from metrics with weights and optimization directions.
terms.prompt_hint: State metric direction per term (max/min).

terms.definition: Set of non-dominated candidates across multiple objectives.
terms.prompt_hint: Use when objectives conflict and weighted tradeoff is uncertain.

terms.definition: Sequence similarity search to assess uniqueness/off-target risk.
terms.prompt_hint: Ask for target catalog (genome/helper), max hits, and task mode.

terms.definition: Interval-based external evidence projected onto anchored sequence.
terms.prompt_hint: Not all BED records are regulatory; keep neutral TRACK grouping.

terms.definition: Continuous signal track (coverage/enrichment) projected on sequence.
terms.prompt_hint: Use for quantitative context; convert to intervals only when needed.

terms.definition: Per-request mode for agent actions: chat-only, ask-before-run, auto.
terms.prompt_hint: Always require explicit user choice for risky operations.
